  "findings": [
    "Isolated metal parts are not properly bonded to the equipment grounding conductor system, creating a shock and electrocution hazard."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "De-energize the equipment and apply Lockout/Tagout (LOTO) procedures.",
    "Install code-compliant bonding jumpers to connect all isolated metal parts to the equipment grounding system."
  ],
  "rationale": "Isolated metal parts that are likely to become energized must be bonded to the equipment grounding conductor to ensure a low-impedance path for fault current, facilitating the operation of overcurrent protective devices. Leaving them isolated during active service work poses an immediate shock hazard to personnel.",
  "references": [
    "NEC 250.104",
    "NEC 250.30"
  ]
}
